scope: All Paediatrics questions with difficulty >= 4 across questions_paeds.json and batches/paeds_*.json (including obgyn-batch-emerg-006 moved into paeds_user_paste_emergency_2026-05-20.json). 145 questions in scope.
definition: Difficulty = cognitive work required to land on the correct answer, not topic rarity. Flag stems whose telegraph phrasing hands the answer to anyone reading carefully.

verdict_codes.relabel: recognition of a complex topic only; downgrade L4/L5 to L1-L2
verdict_codes.rewrite: close to legitimate L4-L5 but a single telegraph sentence pre-commits the answer; remove it and the difficulty rating stands

verdicts.reasoning_required: multi-step: recognise refractory severe asthma after full inhaled bronchodilator + IV steroid course, choose IV magnesium over IV salbutamol/aminophylline/intubation as the next AU paeds step-up
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. No telegraph. Distractor space includes plausible IV salbutamol and aminophylline.

verdicts.telegraph_to_remove: (this hooks the discharge-home and urinalysis-only distractors, since neonatal age alone mandates full septic workup regardless of source)
verdicts.reasoning_required: pattern-match only: any febrile neonate <28 days = full septic workup including LP and triple empiric abx
verdicts.note: An author meta-comment is sitting inside the stem and literally states the rule the answer relies on. Even after removing it, the question is recognition-level for end-of-MD; relabel to L2. If kept as L4 the in-stem rationale must be excised.

verdicts.reasoning_required: multi-step: meningitis Sx + VP shunt (add vancomycin for staph), focal seizures + drowsiness (defer LP, image first), dex with or before first abx dose, neurosurgery for shunt; maternal pen-urticaria is not anaphylaxis so ceftriaxone still fine
verdicts.note: Genuine L5: four independent decisions stacked, each with a plausible distractor.

verdicts.reasoning_required: multi-step bundle: NBM + free-drain OG + triple abx with metronidazole + fluid bolus + surgical referral; selecting the complete AU NEC bundle over partial bundles
verdicts.note: Diagnosis itself is pattern-match (preterm + bilious aspirate + bloody stool + distension = NEC) but L4 hinges on choosing the full management bundle vs partial-credit distractors.

verdicts.reasoning_required: multi-step AU paeds DKA pathway: 10 mL/kg bolus (not 20), deficit over 48h (not 24), insulin at 0.05 unit/kg/hr (not 0.1), delay insulin start; cerebral oedema avoidance
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. AU-specific cut-offs are exactly the cognitive work.

verdicts.reasoning_required: multi-step refractory status: recognise failed 2x benzo, pick second-line (LEV vs PHT vs VPA), VPA excluded (already on it), PHT cardiac concerns; simultaneously prep RSI with thiopentone
verdicts.note: Genuine L5. Several anti-patterns (more midazolam, PHT bolus, no airway plan) act as plausible distractors.

verdicts.reasoning_required: AU-specific modified Jones criteria for high-risk population, plus full secondary prophylaxis register + IM benzathine + aspirin + cardiology bundle
verdicts.note: Pattern recognition for the diagnosis is easy, but choosing 'definite' vs 'probable' under modified Jones and including the secondary prophylaxis register pushes it to legitimate L4. Borderline; keep.

verdicts.reasoning_required: AU-specific NAI workup bundle (skeletal survey + CT then MRI + ophthalmology + mandatory notification) selected over partial bundles
verdicts.note: Diagnosis is recognition (torn frenulum + bruises in pre-mobile infant + inconsistent Hx) but selecting the complete AU bundle over partial-credit distractors is real work.

verdicts.reasoning_required: multi-step refractory anaphylaxis: 2 IM doses failed -> IV adrenaline infusion at AU paeds dose 0.1 microg/kg/min; choose over third IM, glucagon, intubation-first
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Stem explicitly excludes IM technique error so the candidate must escalate.

verdicts.reasoning_required: fluid-refractory septic shock at 60 mL/kg ceiling with signs of fluid overload (hepatomegaly, bibasal crackles) -> peripheral adrenaline (now AU first-line) + prep intubation, not more fluid
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Anti-pattern: another fluid bolus. AU paeds sepsis 2024 guideline favours peripheral adrenaline early.

verdicts.reasoning_required: AU NAI workup bundle for pre-mobile infant with TEN-4 bruising pattern (ear, face, scalp); choose complete bundle over partial
verdicts.note: Recognition of suspected NAI is easy; selecting the full AU bundle (skeletal survey + CT + ophthalmology + mandatory report + child protection consult) is where the L4 reasoning sits.

verdicts.reasoning_required: post-ROSC drowning bundle aligned with ANZCOR: lung-protective ventilation with PEEP, controlled rewarming target 36 degC (not aggressive cooling), post-arrest neuroprotection, ECMO retrieval discussion
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Anti-patterns: hyperventilate to PaCO2 30, therapeutic hypothermia to 32, immediate extubation.

verdicts.telegraph_to_remove: The Australian paracetamol nomogram has a single treatment line starting at 150 mg/L at 4 hours, dropping to 75 mg/L at 8 hours and 37 mg/L at 12 hours
verdicts.reasoning_required: level above the nomogram line at 4.5 h post-ingestion + dose >200 mg/kg -> two-bag NAC (current AU regimen) over three-bag
verdicts.note: Stem hands the nomogram values to the candidate, which removes the main piece of recall work. Remaining reasoning (two-bag vs three-bag, timing of NAC) is solid L4 but not L5. Either remove the in-stem nomogram quotation and keep at L5, or accept L4 and leave as-is.

verdicts.telegraph_to_remove: Calculated Westley score is 6, placing her in the moderate category.
verdicts.reasoning_required: after telegraph removed: calculate Westley from clinical signs, then select moderate-croup bundle (neb adrenaline + dex + 4h obs)
verdicts.note: Stem pre-classifies severity for the candidate. Without that sentence, the candidate has to compute Westley from the described signs and pick the right tier. Even then this is recognition-with-one-calculation, L2-L3 at most. If kept as L4, the severity-class sentence must go.

verdicts.reasoning_required: uncontrolled paeds asthma on low-dose ICS with good adherence and technique -> step up to ICS+LABA (AU step-3) rather than double ICS, LTRA, or OCS course
verdicts.note: AU/GINA paeds step-up between competing options is real work; technique-confirmed + adherence-confirmed deliberately rules out the easy distractors.

verdicts.reasoning_required: pattern-match pertussis (apnoea + paroxysmal + lymphocytosis + unimmunised + maternal cough) -> recall full AU public-health bundle (admit + azithromycin + notify + household chemoprophylaxis)
verdicts.note: Stem stacks every textbook pertussis cue. Diagnosis is pure pattern-match; bundle selection is recall. Closer to L3 than L4.

verdicts.reasoning_required: recall content of NAC asthma action plan 'yellow' zone
verdicts.note: Pure recall of NAC action plan template. No reasoning chain. L2 at most.

verdicts.reasoning_required: CF pulmonary exacerbation criteria (sputum change + FEV1 drop) + dual anti-pseudomonal cover guided by recent sensitivities (ceftazidime + tobramycin) + AU-specific paeds dosing
verdicts.note: The stem hands a clean sensitivity panel and a single dominant organism (Pseudomonas), which collapses the antibiotic choice. Solid L4 but not L5; no genuinely competing pathway to weigh.

verdicts.reasoning_required: Gillick competence assessment + Victorian Abortion Law Reform Act 2008 framework + assess mandatory-reporting threshold (16yo boyfriend, consensual, non-coercive = below threshold) + confidentiality vs encouraging parental involvement
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Four parallel decisions, each with a plausible wrong-direction distractor.

verdicts.reasoning_required: Victorian statutory triple pathway: Child Protection notification + Police + Reportable Conduct Scheme via the school (Commission for Children and Young People)
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Selecting all three pathways simultaneously is the AU/Victoria-specific work.

verdicts.telegraph_to_remove: The treating team is aware that a torn frenulum in a non-mobile infant is regarded as a sentinel injury and is planning the formal NAI workup according to RCH and Victorian Forensic Paediatric Medical Service guidance.
verdicts.reasoning_required: after telegraph removed: recognise sentinel injury in non-mobile infant + recall the AU complete workup including the 11-14 day repeat skeletal survey
verdicts.note: Stem already classifies the case as a sentinel injury and references the specific guidance. Without that sentence the Q is recognition + bundle recall, sits at L3.

verdicts.telegraph_to_remove: smooth philtrum, a thin upper vermilion border, and short palpebral fissures ... significant scatter and specific deficits in three of the recognised neurodevelopmental domains (executive function, adaptive behaviour, language) ... documented to have consumed alcohol at hazardous levels throughout the pregnancy
verdicts.reasoning_required: pattern-match: stem lists 3 sentinel facial features + 3 neurodevelopmental-domain deficits + confirmed PAE, all required for the AU Guide FASD diagnostic category, and the correct option re-states them
verdicts.note: Stem hands every diagnostic criterion verbatim. Pure recognition; not L4.

verdicts.reasoning_required: MARSIPAN-AU admission criteria applied to multiple parameters (HR 38, BMI 14.8, K 3.0, QTc 470), then choose medical ward vs ICU vs psych vs outpatient + refeeding rate (20 kcal/kg/day) to avoid refeeding syndrome
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The disposition and refeeding-rate choice is real work even given the obvious admission threshold.

verdicts.reasoning_required: post-CBT failure paeds MDD -> fluoxetine first-line (AU/NICE), starting dose 10 mg, weekly review in first 4 weeks per TGA boxed warning, CBT continued
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The Q requires both the agent choice and the AU-specific monitoring schedule; distractors include sertraline first-line, fluoxetine without weekly review, etc.

verdicts.reasoning_required: RCH Australian Standards of Care: persistent + insistent + consistent dysphoria, Tanner stage 4 with menarche, MDT in place, family supportive -> GnRH analogue (stage 1 puberty suppression), not gender-affirming hormones (stage 2) and not surgery
verdicts.note: Stem hands every eligibility criterion (Tanner stage, MDT, mental-health stability, capacity, parental support). Real but single-stage decision; L4 fits better than L5.

verdicts.reasoning_required: AU Resuscitation Council post-anaphylaxis observation criteria: >=2 adrenaline doses + asthma + transient collapse triggers extended 6-12 h monitored obs, plus full discharge bundle (action plan, 2 in-date AAIs, ICS optimisation, immunology referral)
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The competing 4-hour standard-obs distractor is plausible; selection requires recognising the severity-feature combination.

verdicts.telegraph_to_remove: C4 sent earlier is reported as low
verdicts.reasoning_required: HAE pattern (non-itchy non-erythematous swelling + family history of throat-swelling death + no response to adrenaline/steroid/antihistamine + low C4) -> C1 inhibitor antigenic + function + SERPING1; treat with C1-INH concentrate or icatibant
verdicts.note: Diagnosis is heavily telegraphed (every classical HAE feature including the low C4 result in the stem). Management bundle is then recall. L3 fits better; if kept at L4 the C4 result should be withheld.

verdicts.reasoning_required: DRESS pattern recognition (carbamazepine ~5 weeks + fever + facial oedema + morbilliform rash + LN + eosinophilia + transaminitis + atypical lymphocytes = RegiSCAR criteria) -> stop drug, steroid, admit, avoid aromatic AEDs
verdicts.note: Stem stacks every textbook DRESS feature. Diagnosis is pure pattern-match; bundle is recall. L3.

verdicts.reasoning_required: chronic spontaneous urticaria on standard-dose H1 with partial benefit -> up-titrate to 4x dose (EAACI/AU step 2) before omalizumab (step 3) or ciclosporin (step 4); rule out OCS as long-term option
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Distractors include several plausible but wrong-step options (OCS, omalizumab first, sedating H1 nocturnally).

verdicts.reasoning_required: necrotising fasciitis / strep TSS over varicella: pain out of proportion + dusky skin + sepsis -> resuscitate, broad-spec IV abx including clindamycin for toxin suppression, urgent surgical source control; ruling out oral cefalexin + topical mupirocin and ruling out delay for MRI
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The competing oral-abx distractor and MRI-first distractor are explicitly listed in the stem, forcing the candidate to commit.

verdicts.reasoning_required: SJS/TEN diagnosis (Bactrim ~9d + atypical targetoid + mucositis + Nikolsky + ~18% BSA) -> cease drug, transfer to paeds burns/ICU, supportive bundle
verdicts.note: Stem stacks every textbook SJS/TEN feature. Diagnosis is pattern-match; management is a known bundle. L4 fits better than L5.

verdicts.reasoning_required: LQTS pattern (exertional/aquatic syncope + family aquatic SCD + QTc 510 + notched T) -> propranolol + exercise restriction + cascade screening
verdicts.note: Stem stacks LQT1-typical triggers, family Hx, prolonged QTc, and a Bazett-corrected value already calculated for the candidate. Recognition + recall.

verdicts.reasoning_required: apply 2020 RHDAustralia high-risk Jones to a case where polyarthritis is the only major and PR prolongation + raised APRs are minors + ASO/anti-DNase B = GAS evidence; distinguish definite vs probable vs not-meeting
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The lead-in explicitly asks which statement correctly applies the criteria, forcing structured application not just pattern recall.

verdicts.reasoning_required: pattern-match Noonan: short stature + dev delay + hypertelorism + downslanting fissures + low-set posteriorly rotated ears + webbed neck + pectus + pulmonary stenosis
verdicts.note: Every Noonan textbook feature is named in the stem. Pure recognition; not L4.

verdicts.reasoning_required: revised Ghent Marfan recognition + recall management bundle (beta-blocker or ARB, sport restriction, surveillance echo + ophthalmology, FBN1 cascade)
verdicts.note: Diagnosis is fully telegraphed (Steinberg, Walker-Murdoch, ectopia lentis, aortic root Z 3.8, family dissection). Bundle is recall. L3.

verdicts.reasoning_required: Fragile X pattern (long face + large ears + macroorchidism + hyperextensible joints + ID + ASD features + ADHD) -> FMR1 PCR with methylation
verdicts.note: Every textbook Fragile X feature is named including macroorchidism. Pure recognition + recall of confirmatory test. Not L5; L2-L3.

verdicts.reasoning_required: eczema herpeticum pattern (HSV contact + punched-out monomorphic umbilicated vesicles on eczematous background + fever + miserable) -> admit, IV aciclovir, urgent ophthalmology
verdicts.note: Diagnosis is heavily telegraphed (grandfather cold-sore contact + classic morphology). Bundle is recall. L3.

verdicts.reasoning_required: Hirschsprung pattern (delayed meconium >48 h + bilious vomiting + explosive release on DRE + paucity of rectal gas) -> surgical referral, suction rectal biopsy
verdicts.note: Every classical Hirschsprung feature is named. Pattern-match + recall of confirmatory investigation. L3.

verdicts.reasoning_required: AU paeds ADHD pharmacotherapy: choose stimulant class (methylphenidate vs lisdex) over atomoxetine/clonidine, AU paeds starting dose (5 mg BD), authorisation rules + side-effect monitoring
verdicts.note: Diagnosis is settled by the stem; the work is the AU-specific medication choice + dose + Schedule 8 authorisation context. Genuine L4.

verdicts.reasoning_required: weigh admit vs outpatient: passive SI, no plan/intent/means, supportive family, no prior attempts -> outpatient safety plan + means restriction + CAMHS within timeframe; distractors include admit to adult psych and CAMHS-only without same-day safety plan
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Real disposition decision with several plausible wrong-direction options.

verdicts.reasoning_required: stratify severe AN as high refeeding-syndrome risk based on BMI 14 + biochemical findings + duration -> 5-10 kcal/kg/day (cautious tier, lower than the 20 kcal/kg used in lower-risk cases), prophylactic phosphate + thiamine, daily electrolyte monitoring, MH referral
verdicts.note: Genuine L5: the refeeding-rate selection requires risk-stratification (high vs standard), and the 5-10 kcal/kg tier is the AU MARSIPAN-AU answer for severe risk. Distinct from paeds-adol-010 (20 kcal/kg).

verdicts.reasoning_required: post-2-benzo refractory paeds status -> second-line AED via APLS: levetiracetam 40 mg/kg vs phenytoin 20 mg/kg vs more midazolam; AU paeds dosing
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Lead-in references the algorithm but the candidate still has to choose between three plausible second-line agents.

verdicts.reasoning_required: refractory severe asthma after full inhaled course + oral steroid -> IV magnesium first, then IV salbutamol/aminophylline; AU paeds dosing 50 mg/kg max 2 g
verdicts.note: Mirrors paeds-002; genuine L4.

verdicts.reasoning_required: cerebral oedema in DKA: recognise Cushing response (HTN + bradycardia + falling GCS + sluggish pupils) in setting of falling Na and rapidly falling glucose -> 3% hypertonic saline 5 mL/kg (current AU evidence over mannitol), reduce fluid rate, urgent CT; do not stop insulin
verdicts.note: Genuine L5. Multiple parallel decisions plus the hypertonic-vs-mannitol AU update. Recognition is non-trivial because the candidate must integrate biochem trends with vitals.

verdicts.reasoning_required: staggered paracetamol ingestion (>4 h between doses) -> nomogram does not apply, treat empirically with two-bag NAC because total dose >150 mg/kg; distractor is plotting the level on the nomogram and deciding 'below line'
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The classic exam anti-pattern (using the nomogram for staggered ingestions) is the dominant distractor.

verdicts.telegraph_to_remove: Bedside echo by the visiting paediatric cardiology fellow on tele-link demonstrates a critical coarctation of the aorta with a closing ductus arteriosus.
verdicts.reasoning_required: after telegraph removed: recognise duct-dependent systemic-flow lesion from pre/post-ductal sat gradient + leg-arm BP gradient + grey collapse, then start PGE1
verdicts.note: Stem hands the diagnosis and the closing-duct mechanism. Without that sentence, recognition + PGE1 recall puts it at L2-L3, not L4.

verdicts.reasoning_required: cerebral oedema in DKA: Cushing response + falling GCS -> 3% hypertonic 5 mL/kg + reduce fluid rate + CT brain; AU paeds DKA protocol
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Slightly less complex than the L5 emerg-005 variant; rating tier is appropriate.

verdicts.reasoning_required: central vs peripheral precocious puberty distinction: GnRH (or analogue) stimulation test over basal LH/FSH, bone age, MRI alone; ordering investigations in the right sequence
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Investigation-choice question with multiple plausible competitors.

verdicts.reasoning_required: Graves' pattern (goitre with bruit + proptosis + lid lag + suppressed TSH + raised T4/T3) -> carbimazole + propranolol + monitor FBC/LFT
verdicts.note: Diagnosis is fully telegraphed (every textbook Graves feature). Bundle is recall. L3.

verdicts.reasoning_required: central DI in LCH (polyuria + polydipsia + Na 148 + raised osm + LCH history) -> desmopressin trial + pituitary MRI
verdicts.note: Diagnosis is heavily telegraphed (LCH known to cause central DI; classic biochem). Investigation is recall.

verdicts.reasoning_required: AU/AAP T21 atlantoaxial guidance: asymptomatic + normal neuro exam -> clinical screen, no routine cervical X-ray; counsel that trampolining is contraindicated
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The current AU position (no routine imaging) is a guideline change that the L4 rating tracks; X-ray-first is the dominant wrong-direction distractor.

verdicts.reasoning_required: Williams syndrome hypercalcaemia: stop vitamin D-containing supplement, switch to low-calcium vitamin D-free formula, endocrine input; avoid thiazide; avoid IV calcium
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Williams-specific management is a non-trivial recall combined with several plausible wrong moves.

verdicts.reasoning_required: Marfan management: Z-score 3.2 with progression -> commence beta-blocker (or losartan) now (not 'observe'), restrict competitive isometric/contact sport, 6-month imaging not annual
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Real medication-initiation threshold + sport-restriction nuances + surveillance interval all in one decision.

verdicts.reasoning_required: vEDS recognition (sister with confirmed COL3A1 + translucent skin + facial features + spontaneous pneumothorax + Beighton 7) -> cascade COL3A1 + non-invasive imaging + celiprolol + pregnancy counselling
verdicts.note: Dx is heavily anchored by the sister's confirmed pathogenic variant. Management bundle (celiprolol specifically, not beta-blocker generic) carries the residual difficulty. L4.

verdicts.reasoning_required: infantile spasms + hypsarrhythmia + TSC2-confirmed -> vigabatrin first-line (TSC-specific) rather than the usual ACTH/prednisolone default
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The TSC override is the key piece; vigabatrin vs steroid is the live distractor.

verdicts.reasoning_required: bilious vomiting in newborn = malrotation with possible midgut volvulus until proven otherwise -> urgent upper GI contrast (within 1 h), surgical team
verdicts.note: Pure recognition + recall of the urgent investigation. The 'within the next hour' urgency framing pushes it to L3 but not L4.

verdicts.reasoning_required: advanced medical NEC (pneumatosis + portal venous gas, no free gas) -> NBM + OG drainage + triple abx + TPN + serial films + surgical involvement, not surgery now
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Distinguishing medical-NEC bundle from immediate-surgery option (which would be wrong without free gas) is the real work.

verdicts.reasoning_required: suspected coeliac in toddler (faltering growth + steatorrhoea + sibling with coeliac on gluten) -> continue gluten, refer for duodenal biopsy, do NOT start gluten-free pre-biopsy; baseline nutritional studies; cascade family screen
verdicts.note: Real anti-pattern (early gluten-free) is the dominant distractor. Solid L4; not quite L5 because dx is pattern-match.

verdicts.reasoning_required: SCD + parvovirus exposure (brother with malar rash) + Hb drop with collapsed reticulocyte count (12 vs baseline 300) -> aplastic crisis from parvovirus
verdicts.note: Stem hands every classical aplastic crisis cue including the absent reticulocyte response. Pure recognition.

verdicts.reasoning_required: SCID pattern (recurrent thrush + PCP + CMV + lymphopaenia + low Ig + absent thymic shadow + family Hx) -> same-day immunology, lymphocyte subsets, isolation, withhold further live vaccines (already had BCG + rotavirus)
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The 'no further live vaccines' + isolation + same-day path together are the AU SCID emergency framework; distractors include outpatient referral and 'await blood film'.

verdicts.reasoning_required: HSV encephalitis pattern (fever + altered mental status + focal seizure + dysphasia + olfactory hallucinations + immunocompetent) -> empirical IV aciclovir at AU paeds 500 mg/m^2 BEFORE LP/MRI complete; not waiting on imaging
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The 'empirical before confirmatory' decision is the live distractor (vs 'await LP', 'await MRI').

verdicts.reasoning_required: paeds latent TB from close household contact + positive Mantoux + normal CXR + asymptomatic -> isoniazid 10 mg/kg + pyridoxine for 9 months (AU paeds regimen) over 4-month rifampicin alternative
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. AU regimen choice and duration plus pyridoxine cover; not pure recognition.

verdicts.reasoning_required: ALL on chemo + face-to-face varicella exposure + no immunity -> VZIG IM within 96 h (extends to 10 d under updated guidance); distractor pathway is IVIG, prophylactic aciclovir, observe
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The time-window cutoff + choice of VZIG over alternatives is the AU/ASCIA reasoning.

verdicts.reasoning_required: febrile neutropenia + septic shock + Hickman line -> pip-taz + gentamicin first, add vancomycin if line infection suspected or shock persists
verdicts.note: Once recognition is settled (febrile neutropenia + line + shock), regimen is recall. Solid L4, not L5.

verdicts.reasoning_required: suspected septic hip: aspirate under GA + send synovial fluid + then commence empirical IV flucloxacillin AFTER sampling; reject pre-tap antibiotics, MRI-first, oral abx outpatient
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The 'tap first, then antibiotics' anti-pattern is the dominant exam point.

verdicts.reasoning_required: acute mastoiditis after failed amoxicillin -> admit, IV ceftriaxone (not flucloxacillin alone), urgent CT temporal bones with contrast, urgent ENT for cortical mastoidectomy + myringotomy
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Antibiotic-class selection + imaging + surgical pathway. The flucloxacillin-only distractor is plausible without the mastoiditis-specific knowledge.

verdicts.reasoning_required: leukocoria + reduced visual behaviour -> urgent paeds ophthalmology referral within 24-48 h for EUA to exclude retinoblastoma
verdicts.note: Pure pattern-match + recall of the AU urgent-referral timeframe. Not L4.

verdicts.reasoning_required: neonatal hyperacute purulent conjunctivitis with corneal involvement + unscreened mother -> investigate (Gram, culture, GC/CT PCR) + IV ceftriaxone/cefotaxime + saline irrigation + urgent paeds ophthalmology; reject topical-only and oral-azithromycin-only
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The IV-systemic over topical + investigation bundle is the AU exam point.

verdicts.reasoning_required: paeds septic hip + sepsis pathway: 10 mL/kg crystalloid (AU 2024 paeds bolus ceiling reduced from 20), tap before abx, flucloxacillin + cefazolin for Kingella in <5 yr, urgent hip washout under GA
verdicts.note: Genuine L5. The reduced bolus volume (10 not 20) + Kingella add-on cefazolin + tap-first ordering stack three independent AU-specific decisions.

verdicts.reasoning_required: NEC in 28-week preterm day 7 -> NBM + large-bore OG free drainage + triple antibiotic cover with metronidazole + resuscitate + surgical review; select complete bundle over partial
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Mirrors paeds-011; recognition is easy but the full bundle is the discriminator.

verdicts.reasoning_required: congenital CMV pattern (SGA + microcephaly + petechiae + HSM + periventricular calcifications + refer OAE) -> valganciclovir 16 mg/kg BD x 6 months with FBC/LFT/audiology monitoring
verdicts.note: Diagnosis is fully telegraphed. Treatment regimen is recall.

verdicts.reasoning_required: HIE eligibility for therapeutic hypothermia: cord pH 6.92 + base deficit 18 + Apgar criteria + Sarnat moderate/severe -> whole-body cooling 33-34 degC for 72 h, aEEG, rewarm 12 h, phenobarbitone for seizures
verdicts.note: Genuine L5. Eligibility criteria + cooling parameters + AED choice + rewarming protocol stack four AU-specific decisions.

verdicts.reasoning_required: PPHN pattern (meconium aspiration + pre/post-ductal sat gradient + echo confirms right-to-left across PDA + high RVSP) -> iNO 20 ppm, reassess OI
verdicts.note: Stem hands the echo diagnosis. Pattern + recall.

verdicts.reasoning_required: term neonate with sudden focal seizure at 36 h: phenobarbitone + broad workup (sepsis + metabolic + LP + urgent MRI); rule out narrow workups
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Distractor space includes 'observe', 'EEG first', 'CT first' (rather than MRI), 'IV calcium only'.

verdicts.reasoning_required: 32-week preterm on CPAP with moderate RDS -> LISA (less invasive surfactant administration) over intubate-surfactant-extubate or InSurE; AU preferred per recent evidence
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Selection between surfactant strategies is a real 2024 AU-practice update.

verdicts.reasoning_required: select which measles contact gets NHIG vs MMR: apply contraindication grid (pregnancy, immunocompromise, age <6 mo) and 6-day NHIG window across 5 named contacts
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Applied selection across multiple contacts is the real work.

verdicts.reasoning_required: ALL on induction + face-to-face varicella + no immunity + within 96 h -> VZIG IM; reject IVIG, prophylactic aciclovir, observe
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. Mirrors paeds-id-012; AU/ASCIA PEP pathway.

verdicts.reasoning_required: non-immune-status-unknown pregnant contact after parvovirus B19 exposure -> maternal IgG/IgM serology first
verdicts.note: Single-step recall. Not L4.

verdicts.reasoning_required: lamotrigine + VPA combo + early rash with Nikolsky + mucositis + ~8% BSA = SJS -> cease lamotrigine + admit paeds burns/HDU + ophthalmology + dermatology
verdicts.note: Diagnosis is heavily telegraphed (lamotrigine + VPA + every textbook SJS feature). Bundle is recall.

verdicts.reasoning_required: necrotising fasciitis with strep TSS post-varicella + shock -> resuscitate, triple-cover IV ceftriaxone + vancomycin + clindamycin (toxin suppression) + urgent surgical debridement; AU paeds sepsis pathway
verdicts.note: Genuine L5. The triple-cover including clindamycin specifically for toxin suppression + emergent surgery + AU paeds sepsis bundles three independent decisions; more severe than the L4 allergy-derm variant.

verdicts.reasoning_required: recurrent paeds UTI with normal US -> DMSA at 4-6 months to look for cortical scarring; reject MCUG (no reflux features), repeat US, observe
verdicts.note: Genuine L4. The selection between DMSA, MCUG and observation under current AU paeds UTI imaging guidance is real.

verdicts.reasoning_required: PUV with distended bladder + obstructive AKI + sepsis features -> immediate urethral catheterisation (small feeding tube) before US, before fluid bolus, before surgical consult
